title: China Bought 90% of Iran's Oil. The 'Maximum Pressure' Campaign Is Dead.
narrative: The Trump administration says its "maximum pressure" sanctions regime is effectively choking Iran's oil revenues. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent accused China of financing Iran's military by purchasing 90% of its oil. The administration has issued sweeping sanctions against Chinese refiners and warned global banks of secondary sanctions risk.
findings: China's Commerce Ministry issued a formal directive invoking a 2021 "blocking statute" ordering Chinese companies to defy US sanctions on Iranian oil
Treasury's own accusation confirms China is now openly acting as Iran's primary economic shield
Maritime intelligence from Windward: 146 of 167 vessels in the Strait of Hormuz are going "dark" (disabling AIS tracking)
Satellite/HSI data confirms covert loading activity continues at Iran's Kharg Island main export hub despite blockade
Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi was in Beijing during the week of May 11 for talks with Wang Yi
Trump considering lifting sanctions on Chinese firms as part of a "Grand Bargain" to end the war
telemetry: Chinese crude imports from Iran reportedly up 23% year-over-year through Q1 2026
146 of 167 vessels in Strait of Hormuz going dark — systematic industrial-scale evasion
Kharg Island export activity confirmed by satellite imagery through May 2026
verdict: The 'maximum pressure' campaign has failed. China openly defied US sanctions with a state directive. The 'Grand Bargain' leak suggests the endgame is rewarding that defiance with sanctions relief — meaning either the campaign was never real, or it's being quietly buried.
